perturbation.explanation: The "Effective Date" is changed from February 16, 1998, to March 1, 1998. This creates a contradiction because numerous sections, such as those related to the Term and Conversion timelines, explicitly reference the original Effective Date. Changing it without updating those sections creates ambiguity about when contractual obligations begin. For example, any clauses referring to actions 'as of the Effective Date' are now unclear.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The initial term of this Agreement shall be the Term, unless extended or earlier terminated in accordance with this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'February 16, 1998' to 'March 1, 1998' for the Effective Date directly contradicts any clause that depends on the original date for calculating timelines or obligations. While the contradicted text provided only refers to the Term, the explanation clearly states that the effective date is crucial for other sections such as conversion timelines. This creates a direct conflict within the document because those timelines are now unclear.

perturbation.original_text: K. "Damages" shall mean all direct, actual and verifiable losses, liabilities, damages and claims and related costs and expenses (including reasonable attorneys' fees and court costs, costs of investigation, litigation, settlement, judgment, interest and penalties) but excluding any and all consequential, incidental, punitive and exemplary damages.
perturbation.changed_text: K. "Damages" shall mean all losses, liabilities, damages and claims and related costs and expenses.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of 'Damages' is changed to omit the specifications regarding 'direct, actual and verifiable losses' and the exclusion of 'consequential, incidental, punitive and exemplary damages.' This is contradictory, because Section 13.2 'No Consequential Damages' specifically refers to these types of damages. By removing this definition, Section 13.2 becomes unclear and unenforceable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Neither Customer nor M&I shall be liable for, nor will the measure of any damages in any event include, any indirect, incidental, punitive, special or consequential damages or amounts for loss of income, profits or savings arising out of or relating to performance or non-performance under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the limitations on what constitutes 'Damages', specifically removing the exclusion of 'consequential, incidental, punitive and exemplary damages'. The contradicted text (Section 13.2) explicitly states that neither party is liable for these types of damages. By removing the exclusion from the definition of 'Damages', the changed text directly contradicts Section 13.2, creating uncertainty and a strong possibility of unenforceability of Section 13.2. The original text definition was compatible with Section 13.2.

perturbation.original_text: U. "Proper Instructions" shall mean the manner in which Customer shall provide instructions to M&I, as set forth in Section 3.3 below.
perturbation.changed_text: U. "Proper Instructions" shall mean instructions provided to M&I.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of "Proper Instructions" is altered to remove the explicit reference to Section 3.3, which details the approved methods for providing instructions. This creates an in-text contradiction because Section 3.3 elaborates on what constitutes 'Proper Instructions,' but without the defined connection, there is ambiguity about the validity of instructions, making it challenging to determine if instructions were properly given. Also the sentence in section 3.3 'M&I shall be deemed to have received "Proper Instructions" upon receipt of written or oral instructions which M&I believes in good faith to be signed or given by any person(s) whose name(s) and signature(s) are listed on the most recent certificate delivered by Customer to M&I which lists those persons authorized to give orders, corrections and instructions in the name of and on behalf of Customer.' become contratictory.
perturbation.contradicted_text: A. M&I shall be deemed to have received "Proper Instructions" upon receipt of written or oral instructions which M&I believes in good faith to be signed or given by any person(s) whose name(s) and signature(s) are listed on the most recent certificate delivered by Customer to M&I which lists those persons authorized to give orders, corrections and instructions in the name of and on behalf of Customer. B. Proper Instructions shall specify the action requested to be taken or omitted. Proper Instructions shall include instructions sent to M&I or its agent(s) by letter, memorandum, telegram, cable, telex, telecopy facsimile, video (CRT) terminal or other "on-line" system, or similar means of communication, or given orally over the telephone or in person by a person authorized by Customer pursuant to Section 3.4(A) to provide Proper Instructions. Proper Instructions shall include any file transmission received by M&I from Customer, or any agent of Customer who is thereof authorized in writing.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the explicit link to Section 3.3, which defines how 'Proper Instructions' are given. This creates a direct conflict with the contradicted text, specifically section A, which refers to the receipt of 'Proper Instructions' and the authorization of individuals to give them. Without the connection to Section 3.3 established in the original definition, it becomes ambiguous whether the instructions received by M&I were indeed 'Proper Instructions' according to the contract, making the contradicted section unenforceable as written. The change falls under the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' perturbation definition.
